work: Zalizniak, Грамматический очерк санскрита (Grammatical Essay of Sanskrit), 1978
synthesis:
FULL DRAIN (74 verified; after the H978 + H1000 + H1001 + H1004 + H1012 + H1022 instruments: 65 TRUE · 2 OVERSTATED · 1 FALSE · 6 UNTESTABLE — the §68 flagship and its §67 siblings measured by the 1978 column, the §207 style-gradient flagship measured by the period map, the §59 root-shape flagship measured by the root-shape parser, and the §§212-217 syntax cluster measured by the treebank instrument, see OCH-21..OCH-23, OCH-63, OCH-16 and OCH-65..OCH-68). Zaliznyak held ZERO fact-axis flags through 70 of 74 claims — longer than either sibling grammar — until the syntax cluster (§§212-217) became testable 16-07-2026: the treebank instrument found the one absolute quantifier in the book (OCH-68, the ya-...ta- antecedent-clause rule) holds at only 75.2% (n=335, genre-skew-checked), and two of three packed word-order tendencies in OCH-66 plus the §215 coordinate>subordinate claim (OCH-67, genuinely inverted 6.5:1) do not clear a majority either — all THREE flags land in the one domain (syntax) this register could not previously test, not scattered across the book. Where Kochergina's absolutes broke on known exception classes and even Bühler slipped once on a frequency direction, Zaliznyak's quantifiers are engineered everywhere ELSE in the book: two-level hedges that the corpus confirms level by level (OCH-10 — his 'крайне редко' for final ṇ vs plain 'редко' for ṅ/ñ is exactly the order-of-magnitude split, 118 vs 659/1,934), absolutes reserved for structurally safe gaps (OCH-17 no short-a-final roots), enumerated escape hatches ('только as и группа samprasāraṇa, и несколько других'), and surface-frequency statements that measure out at 19:1 in his favour (OCH-29 saḥ→so/sa, 95.1%). The cleanest single contrast is the verb-accent rule (OCH-72): the SAME fact graded Kochergina OVERSTATED for «никогда» (HK-10) grades Zaliznyak TRUE for «как правило» — third data point of the programme's central finding that presentation calibration, not factual accuracy, is where grammars differ — a finding OCH-66/68 now ALSO extend (soft hedges still overreach when the underlying syntactic tendency itself turns out weak, not just when the quantifier is an absolute). On that axis the three books now rank: Zaliznyak (3 flags / 74 = 4.1%) > Bühler (5 / 64 = 7.8%) > Kochergina (12+24 / 234) — still the best-calibrated of the three, no longer flag-free.

The remaining UNTESTABLE entries are the register's second product: a requirements list for the pipeline's next instruments, each named in its entry — ~~period-tagged corpus slices~~ BUILT 16-07-2026 (period_style_gradient.py, H1000: OCH-63 flipped to measured TRUE — the style-I→style-II gradient is real and monotonic on every annotation-robust axis; OCH-47's early-vs-late causative half CLOSED the same day by the causative detector, H1001: the guṇa 'deviation' falls 81% → 26% → 21% веды→эпос→классика among fluctuating roots), ~~a root-shape parser over the Talmud catalog~~ BUILT 16-07-2026 (root_shape_parser.py, H1012: OCH-16 flipped to measured TRUE — 727/745 = 97.6% of the catalog fits the §59 six-slot template, 98.4% of the roots where a nucleus could even be located), ~~a treebank/parsed slice~~ BUILT 16-07-2026 (treebank_syntax_stats.py, H1022: dcs_full.sqlite's own head/deprel columns turned out to carry a genuine, fully-tagged 29,433-sentence UD slice all along — OCH-65 confirmed TRUE, OCH-66/68 flipped OVERSTATED, OCH-67 flipped FALSE, all genre-skew-checked against a classical-vs-Vedic split before verdicting; OCH-69's cross-LANGUAGE comparison against Russian/Latin stays out of scope, no comparable parsed corpus on the other side), compound-type tagging (OCH-58, OCH-60), and the 1978↔2026 morphoclass-series crosswalk (OCH-21..OCH-23). Two dead ends are recorded with their numbers so they are never re-derived as refutations: the naive Talmud series join INVERTS the §68 aniṭ/seṭ×series claim (36.8% aniṭ in the predicted-aniṭ group — but the join equates 1978 and 2026 series names without validation and skips the полноизменяемые filter), and the aggregate i-rate proxy INVERTS the §63 veṭ ranking (tvā 78.1% tops the scale — but composition bias across suffix populations disqualifies the aggregate as a measure of within-root fluctuation).

Reuse note, extending the seed's original point below: beyond the three direct reuses, the drain leaned on the whole programme — eleven entries cross-reference Bühler's register or harvest (the marj/vṛddhi, ṝ→īr/ūr, ar-future-i, gamya/gatya, causative-aorist, desiderative and intensive claims are now each attested in TWO independent grammars and one verdict), one reuses a verified Bühler entry outright (OCH-57 = HB-48, as this book's own harvest header planned), and the Talmud's Приложение-1 catalog served as the D-B morphoclass authority for the first time as DATA (OCH-15/OCH-17/OCH-32) rather than as citation. The original seed synthesis follows.

Six-claim seed (H797 Phase 2, Ocherk). Three claims are DIRECT REUSES of ground truth already computed for other books in this cross-grammar program — a first demonstration that the program's corpus infrastructure genuinely compounds across grammars rather than needing rebuilding each time. OCH-1 (ṝ phoneme rarity) reuses Kochergina's HK-7 vowel census verbatim (ṛ is 126× the combined rare sonants ṝ/ḷ/ḹ) — TRUE, and Zaliznyak's own qualification ("only in certain grammatical forms of ar-stems, §86") is more precise than either Kochergina's or Bühler's phrasing of the same fact. OCH-3 (precative middle almost unattested post-Vedic) reuses Kochergina's HK-39 figure exactly (221 tokens, 0.03% of verbal) — TRUE. OCH-5 (rare -am absolutive) reuses Bühler's already-measured absolutive split (-ya 78.4% / -tvā 19.6% / -am 0.4%) — TRUE, and the three grammars' independent framing of the same fact (Kochergina doesn't discuss it; Bühler calls it "очень редко"; Zaliznyak calls it "изредка") converges on the same corpus reality. OCH-4 (çās as a documented exception to the closed-long-vowel-root defective pattern) cross-confirms the Knauer parse-audit's KN-3 finding (Whitney §639) from a completely different digitized source — the SAME irregular root flagged independently by two authors 30 years and two genres apart. OCH-2 (Class I is the largest present-stem class) is a clean, freshly-computed TRUE (30.34% of verbal tokens, more than double the next-largest thematic class). OCH-6 (thematic-stem formation ranks a > ya > aya by frequency) is TRUE in ranking direction but the margin between ya (12.58%) and aya (12.12%) is thin (0.46 points) — "реже" for aya is real but nowhere near as dramatic a gap as a-suffix's 44.44% dominance, worth flagging as a mild pedagogical understatement of how close the two minority classes actually are. No errors found in this seed; too small and too reuse-biased a sample (selected for verifiability, not randomly) to characterize Zaliznyak's overall calibration — that judgment awaits the 68-candidate backlog, which includes several potentially rich targets (§68's aniṭ/seṭ-by-alternation-series correlation; §207's flagship Vedic-to-Classical syntactic-style diachronic claim) that need new corpus queries this seed didn't build.

claims.claim_ru: Фонема /ṝ/ встречается очень редко (только в некоторых грамматических формах имён на ar, § 86).
claims.number: ṛ 199,930 occurrences (1.75% of monophthong vowels) vs ṝ 1,588 (0.01%) — ṛ is 126× the combined rare sonants ṝ/ḷ/ḹ (DCS-2021 vowel census, reused verbatim from KocherginaUchebnik_1998/claims_dcs_stats.json HK7_vowel_census)
claims.ref: DCS-2021 0.csv vowel census (../KocherginaUchebnik_1998/verify_claims_dcs.py); cf. KocherginaUchebnik_1998 HK-7
claims.note: Zaliznyak's qualification — confined to certain grammatical forms of ar-stem nouns (§86) — is more precise than a bare rarity claim; the corpus figure (126× rarer than ṛ) supports "очень редко" without overstatement. Third independent confirmation of the same underlying DCS fact (after Kochergina's own HK-7 statement of it), now cross-book reused rather than recomputed.

claims.claim_ru: Класс I (самый многочисленный): корень в ступени guṇa.
claims.number: class I = 237,100 tokens (30.34% of 781,541 present-system tokens) — more than double the next-largest class (class II, 154,301, 19.74%) — VisualDCS/verb_classes.json
claims.note: Not a close call — class I outnumbers every other class by a wide margin, confirming the parenthetical without qualification.

claims.claim_ru: В med. (который в послеведийский период почти не встречается) основа прекатива равна основе med. аориста 4 или 5 + ī.
claims.number: 221 tokens = 0.03% of 781,618 verbal tokens ('Benedictive Medium', DCS-2021 — reused verbatim from KocherginaUchebnik_1998/claims_dcs_stats.json HK39_precative_middle)
claims.ref: DCS-2021 timws.csv; cf. KocherginaUchebnik_1998 HK-39 (same figure, independently stated there as 'крайне редко'); Талмуд обогащает — Ā-прекатив по 4 (продуктивно), 5 (реже) И 6 (еще реже) — [Талмуд-сверка H1054](https://github.com/gasyoun/SanskritGrammar/blob/main/TolchelnikovTalmud_2026/AORIST_REGISTRY_TALMUD_CROSSCHECK_2026.md)
claims.note: Second independent grammar making the identical, now-doubly-confirmed rarity claim about the same paradigm cell.

claims.claim_ru: Закрытые корни, содержащие долгую гласную или дифтонг... неполноизменяемые. Исключения: полноизменяемые √çās приказывать (слабая ступень ciṣ) и корни группы √sīv шить.
claims.number: systemic: Whitney §639 documents çās as an irregular reduplicated-type root with a distinct weak-stem çiṣ- (matching Zaliznyak's ciṣ), confirming it is NOT a plain defective long-vowel root
claims.ref: WhitneyGrammar_1889/09_The_Present_System.mdx §639; cf. KnauerFrazy_1908/parse_audit.yml KN-3 (same root, same Whitney citation, confirmed independently from a completely different digitized source — a phrase-reader footnote, not a grammar's exception list)
claims.note: The same irregular root (çās) is flagged independently by Knauer's 1908 phrase-reader footnote (KN-3) and Zaliznyak's 1978 grammar's exception list, 70 years apart, in two completely different genres of source — a clean cross-corpus, cross-genre confirmation rather than a coincidence of citing the same textbook tradition.

claims.claim_ru: Изредка встречается деепричастие на -am; ступень корня --- как в 3 sg act. перфекта. Пример: çrāvam.
claims.number: -am absolutive = 0.4% of distinct absolutive forms, vs -ya 78.4% and -tvā 19.6% (reused verbatim from BuhlerLeitfaden_1923/claims.yml's absolutive-split metric)
claims.ref: DCS-2021 (BuhlerLeitfaden_1923/verify_claims_dcs.py); cf. BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 (same split, called 'очень редко' there vs 'изредка' here — both hedges hold up against the same 0.4% figure)
claims.note: Three grammars, three framings of the same corpus fact: Kochergina doesn't mention the -am absolutive at all; Bühler calls it "очень редкая" (very rare); Zaliznyak calls it "изредка" (occasionally) — a slightly softer hedge than Bühler's, and if anything the more accurate one for a form that is rare but not vanishing (0.4%, not 0.04%).

claims.claim_ru: Тематические основы презенса у первичных глаголов обычно образуются непосредственно от корня с помощью элементов a или ya, реже aya, cha.
claims.number: a-suffix (classes I+VI combined): 347,284 tokens (44.44% of present-system tokens); ya-suffix (class IV): 98,321 (12.58%); aya-suffix (class X): 94,735 (12.12%) — VisualDCS/verb_classes.json
claims.note: The ranking direction (a > ya > aya) holds, but the gap Zaliznyak implies between "обычно" (a/ya) and "реже" (aya) is thin where it matters most: ya (12.58%) and aya (12.12%) are only 0.46 percentage points apart — nowhere near as decisive a split as a-suffix's 44.44% dominance over both. A learner reading "реже" for aya next to "обычно" for ya would expect a much bigger gap than the corpus actually shows between those two specifically.

claims.claim_ru: Анунасика встречается почти исключительно в случаях, указанных в § 43, п. 2.
claims.number: systemic: the nasalized-l outcome of final n + initial l (tāṁl lokān type) is the one living anunāsika context of the classical language; other uses are Vedic/orthographic
claims.ref: Whitney §206 (final n before l); cf. BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 harvest (Урок VI, same rule)
claims.note: 'Почти исключительно' is the right hedge: the n+l junction is where classical anunāsika lives; residual uses are Vedic recitation practice, outside this corpus's scope.

claims.claim_ru: В большинстве индийских школ санскрита ṛ, ṝ, ḷ принято читать соответственно как ri, rī, li (или lri); распространено также чтение ç как ṣ.
claims.number: no instrument: a sociophonetic census of recitation schools is outside every ground-truth source of this pipeline (DCS is a text corpus; Whitney describes the same convention qualitatively)
claims.ref: cf. Whitney §24 (the ri-pronunciation acknowledged as the modern practice)
claims.note: The convention itself is well attested in the descriptive literature (Whitney reports it too); only the quantifier 'в большинстве школ' has no measurable base here. Same honesty class as Kochergina's HK-41/HK-42.

claims.claim_ru: l в конце слова и фразы теоретически возможно, но практически почти не встречается.
claims.number: 838 word-final l tokens of 3,402,414 word tokens = 0.025% (DCS-2021 surface text; verify_claims_dcs.py OCH_final_consonant_census)
claims.note: A theoretically-possible-but-nearly-absent claim now carrying its corpus number — the exact kind of footnote-grade precision Zaliznyak's own text models.
claims.mg_footnote: Перепись конечных согласных по DCS-2021 (3,4 млн словоупотреблений): конечное l — 838 случаев, 0,025 % слов. «Практически почти не встречается» подтверждено числом.

claims.claim_ru: Носовая ṇ перед паузой или гласной, начинающей следующее слово, встречается 'крайне редко'; носовые n̄, ñ перед согласной, начинающей следующее слово, встречаются 'редко'.
claims.number: word-final counts (DCS-2021, 3.4M word tokens): ṇ 118 · ṅ 659 · ñ 1,934 — vs n 83,091 · m 249,461 · ṁ 532,979
claims.note: Even the fine two-level hedge is confirmed: ṇ (118) is an order of magnitude rarer than ṅ/ñ (659/1,934), exactly matching 'крайне редко' vs plain 'редко'.
claims.mg_footnote: Конечные носовые по DCS-2021: ṇ — 118, ṅ — 659, ñ — 1 934 словоупотребления против n — 83 091, m — 249 461, анусвара — 532 979. Двухступенчатая градация Зализняка («крайне редко» для ṇ против «редко» для ṅ/ñ) видна прямо в числах: ṇ на порядок реже ṅ/ñ.

claims.claim_ru: Факультативный переход k, ṭ, t, p перед носовой в носовую соответствующего ряда: 'наиболее обычен такой переход для t', например tat nāma → tan nāma (реже tad nāma).
claims.number: final-stop census (DCS-2021): t 99,262 word-final tokens vs k 4,100 · ṭ 1,088 · p 113 — the t-junction is 24× the next stop's, so its nasal variant is by far the most-encountered one
claims.ref: DCS-2021 0.csv final-consonant census; Whitney §§161, 198 (optional t→n before nasals)
claims.note: The claim is about the observed commonness of the t-variant; the input-frequency census settles the direction without needing junction-level sandhi tagging.
claims.mg_footnote: Частотность конечных смычных по DCS-2021: t — 99 262, k — 4 100, ṭ — 1 088, p — 113. «Наиболее обычен для t» держится уже на входной частоте: стыков с конечным t в 24 раза больше, чем со всеми прочими смычными вместе взятыми.

claims.claim_ru: h после k, ṭ, t, p может переходить (и обычно переходит) соответственно в gh, ḍh, dh, bh, например tat hi → tad dhi (реже tad hi).
claims.number: systemic: Whitney gives the conversion of initial h to the corresponding sonant aspirate after a final mute as the usual treatment, retained h as the minority option
claims.note: The 'обычно... (реже tad hi)' two-sided hedge matches Whitney's description exactly.

claims.claim_ru: Вместо ожидаемого ḥ в ряде случаев выступает звук, требуемый правилами внутренних сандхи (r, s или ṣ)... перед k, kh, p, ph — на стыке основ сложного слова (часто, но не всегда) и на конце элементов nis, dus (почти всегда), например niṣputra-.
claims.number: systemic: Whitney treats compound-seam s as usually kept/retroflexed before k/p (with variation), and niṣ-/duṣ- as the standing forms (niṣputra, duṣkṛta)
claims.ref: Whitney §§172, 185; cf. BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 harvest (Урок XIII, nis+p → niṣ)
claims.note: Source-checked against the mdx (the harvest's paraphrase had inverted the direction — the deviation is s/r/ṣ INSTEAD OF expected ḥ). The two-level hedge (часто-не-всегда vs почти-всегда) matches the reference treatment's split between general seams and nis/dus.

claims.claim_ru: В ряду R2 (слабая ступень īr, ūr): 'Обычно ūr после губных согласных, īr — в прочих случаях.'
claims.number: systemic: pūrṇa (labial) vs tīrṇa, çīrṇa, gīrṇa (non-labial) — the standard ṝ → īr/ūr distribution
claims.ref: Whitney §242; cf. BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 claims (passive ṝ → īr, ūr after labials — same rule, third book)
claims.note: Third grammar in the programme stating the same labial conditioning; all three agree with Whitney.

claims.claim_ru: Норму для санскрита составляют односложные... корни. Неодносложные корни встречаются редко, в основном в заимствованных словах; среди собственно глагольных корней неодносложных почти нет.
claims.number: systemic: the Talmud Приложение-1 catalog (745 roots) and Whitney's root inventory are monosyllabic throughout up to a handful of denominative-like entries — the polysyllabic residue is not of verbal-root stock
claims.note: The root-morphoclass authority (D-B) is the natural adjudicator here; its catalog is effectively 100% monosyllabic.

claims.claim_ru: Структура подавляющего большинства санскритских корней укладывается в следующую схему: (шумная согласная)-(сонант)-чередующийся элемент-(сонант)-(шумная согласная).
claims.number: MEASURED with the root-shape parser built 16-07-2026 (root_shape_parser.py, H1012): over the 745-root Приложение-1 catalog, the nucleus (position 4) is located per the root's own ryad tag using §50's weak/guṇa/vṛddhi table (plus §54's before-vowel allomorphs — some citations show dhāv, not dhau); flanking consonants are tokenized (kṣ and aspirate digraphs count as one unit each, per §59) and classified sonant vs obstruent. **727/745 = 97.6% fit** the six-slot template overall (98.4% of the 739 roots where a nucleus could be identified at all). Named exceptions confirmed present at real but minority scale: irregular v/m in slot 2 (21 roots, e.g. vyath, mlā), slot-1 s+obstruent (22, e.g. sthā, styā), slot-6 obstruent clusters (126, e.g. katth). The 18 residual non-fits are individually documented (not a silent cap): 9 genuine unusual clusters (ts-, ps-, śc-, ṣṭh- onsets; -ll, -rṇ, -rṣy, -nv codas), 6 catalog/tradition citation mismatches (kṣmāi, sphur/sphar/sphṛ), 3 extra-syllable roots (irādh, irajy, palāy)
claims.ref: ZalizniakOcherk_1978/och16_root_shape_stats.json; root_shape_parser.py (15/15 self-test replicating the §'s own worked examples: √i, √nī, √çru, √sthā, √styā, √iṣ, √pad, √krudh, √jīv, √cumb, √kṣṇu, √takṣ, √vyath, √mlā, √katth)
claims.note: 'Подавляющее большинство' (overwhelming majority) is not just directionally plausible but measured at 97.6% — a strong confirmation, with the exceptions he names (kṣ, v/m-in-slot-2, slot-6 clusters, s+stop) all independently attested in the catalog at the scale his hedged wording implies.

claims.claim_ru: Важная особенность: в санскрите нет открытых корней ряда A1.
claims.number: systemic: no Sanskrit root ends in short a — open A-series roots all carry ā (dā, sthā, pā...); Whitney's inventory and the Talmud catalog have no counterexample
claims.note: A safe absolute: the gap is structural (short a in that position would be the alternating element itself, not a root-final).

claims.claim_ru: Подтип-3 (неполноизменяемые корни со ступенью vṛddhi) очень малочислен.
claims.number: systemic: the standing examples are mṛj and a few isolated stems — Whitney treats constant vṛddhi as an irregularity of individual roots, not a class
claims.note: 'Очень малочислен' is if anything generous — the type is nearly a singleton in living use.

claims.claim_ru: Корень √marj вытирать, чистить отличается от обычных полноизменяемых тем, что вместо ступени guṇa у него, как правило, выступает vṛddhi.
claims.number: systemic: mārṣṭi, mārjitum/mārṣṭum, mamārja — the vṛddhi-for-guṇa root par excellence (weak vocalic forms optionally vṛddhied too: mṛjanti/mārjanti)
claims.ref: Whitney §627; = BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-44 (Bühler: 'всегда'; Zaliznyak: 'как правило' — both hold; Zaliznyak's hedge additionally covers the mṛjanti-type weak forms)
claims.note: Cross-grammar convergence with a calibration nuance: Bühler's absolute is safe for strong forms; Zaliznyak's 'как правило' is the more exact scope once weak-form variation counts.

claims.claim_ru: Корни ряда A1 обычно неполноизменяемы, например: √tap, √tyaj. Полноизменяемы здесь только √as быть и группа корней с чередованиями samprasāraṇa, и несколько других.
claims.number: systemic: a-vowel roots generally keep their grade (tap, tyaj type); the alternators are as (as-/s-), han, and the samprasāraṇa set (vac, vad, vap, vas, vah, yaj, svap, grah...) — matching Whitney's irregular-root inventory
claims.note: 'Обычно... только... и несколько других' — hedged, enumerated, and escape-hatched; the reference inventory fits inside it.

claims.claim_ru: Среди открытых корней ряда A2 оба типа [полноизменяемые/неполноизменяемые] представлены примерно поровну.
claims.number: MEASURED with the 1978 crosswalk column (build_1978_crosswalk.py, §§66-67 rules + named lists over 749 roots): open A₂ roots split 19 полноизменяемые / 13 неполноизменяемые / 18 колеблющихся — among the decided, 59/41; with the fluctuating middle counted as §67 itself describes («ряд корней колеблется»), 'примерно поровну' holds
claims.note: UNBLOCKED by the 1978 crosswalk column built 15-07-2026 — the exact instrument this entry named. The split even reproduces the §'s own three-way texture: two near-even named camps and a fluctuating residue.

claims.claim_ru: Корни рядов R, M, N могут быть обоих типов, но большинство употребительных корней — полноизменяемые, например: √darç, √gam, √bandh.
claims.number: MEASURED twice. By type: 139/160 R/M/N root+homonym pairs полноизменяемые (87%). By the STRICT token-weighted replication (och22_token_weighted.py, DCS-2026 SQLite × the 1978 columns, citation-key aliases resolved): 96.8% of the 151,195 simplex R/M/N verbal tokens — 96.6% of 220,228 with preverbed compounds — sit on полноизменяемые roots; the top-20 'употребительные' roots are 19/20 полноизменяемые (only vṛdh defective), top-50 44/50; and the §'s own examples rank #2 (gam, 28,878 tokens), #3 (dṛś, 15,214) and #13 (bandh, 3,526)
claims.note: UNBLOCKED with OCH-21/OCH-23 by the crosswalk instrument, then CLOSED STRICTLY 15-07-2026: the deferred lemma-frequency join keyed to citation forms is built (divergent keys mapped ṛc→arc, bṛh→bṛṃh, jambh→jabh, tark→tarkay, carc→carcay; 14 rare Vedic roots honestly reported unattested, not zero-faked). The token-weighted margin (96.6–96.8%) comes out STRONGER than the 87% type margin — the a-fortiori argument was correct and is now measured."

claims.claim_ru: Из открытых корней полноизменяемые рядов I1, U1, R1, M1, N1, а также I2 обычно относятся (или по крайней мере близки) к типу aniṭ... Прочие открытые корни... обычно относятся к типу seṭ.
claims.number: MEASURED with the PROPER join (1978 crosswalk column: §63's A₂-exclusion honoured, полноизменяемость filter applied, §66's own index assignments for open R/M/N): group A (полноизменяемые open I₁/U₁/R₁/M₁/N₁/I₂) = 31 aniṭ / 44 veṭ / 6 seṭ — aniṭ-or-veṭ («или по крайней мере близки») = 92.6%, plain seṭ just 7.4%; group B (other open non-A-series) = 24 seṭ / 29 veṭ / 4 aniṭ — seṭ-or-veṭ = 93.0%, plain aniṭ just 7.0%. The claimed correlation is emphatic in both directions once the join is faithful to the §'s own terms. The earlier NAIVE-join inversion (36.8% aniṭ in the predicted group, recorded 15-07 as a dead end) is now fully explained: it had equated 2026 ryad with 1978 series (they disagree on open M/N indices — jan is N₂ per §66 but N₁ in the 2026 column), skipped the полноизменяемые filter, and let the §63-excluded ā-roots flood group B with aniṭ
claims.ref: ZalizniakOcherk_1978/och_1978_stats.json OCH23_s68_proper_join; build_1978_crosswalk.py (15/15 validation checks against §§66-68's own named roots)
claims.note: The register's most valuable open item, CLOSED: the structural prediction behind the programme's flagship frequency fact (HK-4/HB-59's -iṣya majority) is real — seṭ/aniṭ membership tracks alternation-series × полноизменяемость almost deterministically at the plain-type level, with veṭ as the fluctuation band Zaliznyak's own hedge reserved. The dead-end pilot stays in history (see the drain-era note in git) as the PALSULE-audit lesson it was.

claims.claim_ru: Смешанный тип (veṭ)... При таких колебаниях i чаще всего появляется перед -sya- и -tar-, реже всего — перед -ta- и -tvā-.
claims.number: AGGREGATE PROXY PILOT, recorded, not trusted: i-rates over ALL distinct forms are sya 56.8% · tar (periphrastic -tā) 42.1% · tavya 51.6% · ta 54.8% · tvā 78.1% — the aggregate puts tvā HIGHEST, inverting the claimed extremes. But the claim is about the WITHIN-ROOT fluctuation of veṭ roots, and the aggregate mixes seṭ-always-i and aniṭ-never-i roots whose lexical composition differs per suffix — composition bias disqualifies the proxy (verify_claims_dcs.py OCH_vet_i_rates)
claims.ref: DCS-2021 15.csv/12.csv; proper test = per-root doublet analysis over Talmud set_code v1..v4 subtypes
claims.note: Second recorded dead end of this drain: the right instrument is a per-veṭ-root doublet census (does the SAME root fluctuate per suffix), joinable via the Talmud's set_code. Until then the claim stays open, with the misleading aggregate documented so nobody re-derives it as a refutation.

claims.claim_ru: Числительные (количественные) изменяются в большинстве случаев только по падежам, некоторые — по падежам и родам.
claims.number: systemic: gendered declension is confined to 1-4 (eka, dva, tri, catur); 5-19 and the tens/hundreds decline for case only
claims.note: The 'некоторые' are exactly the first four numerals — the inventory-true split.

claims.claim_ru: Лишь в редчайших случаях корневое имя склоняется по склонению обычных имен (nar- § 87, han- § 89) или наоборот (strī- § 98).
claims.number: systemic: the named items (nṛ/nar-, -han compounds, strī) are the standing irregulars of the reference description; no broader crossover class exists
claims.note: 'В редчайших случаях' with the full enumeration attached — calibrated and closed.

claims.claim_ru: Очень небольшое число существительных в классическом санскрите несклоняемо; например, svar небо.
claims.number: systemic: indeclinable substantives are a handful of frozen items (svar-type); Whitney treats them under indeclinables, not as a declension class
claims.note: Safe smallness claim about a closed lexical residue.

claims.claim_ru: Чередование ступеней... характерно для несреднего рода; в среднем роде оно представлено лишь в очень незначительной степени.
claims.number: systemic: the strong cases (where the strong stem appears) are N/Acc of the non-neuter; neuter N/V/Acc are weak/middle everywhere except the plural in -i/-āni — so neuter paradigms surface the alternation only there
claims.note: 'В очень незначительной степени' = exactly the neuter N.V.Acc.pl slot; the structural reason is the strong-case definition itself.

claims.claim_ru: В силу сандхи это saḥ реально предстает чаще всего в виде so или sa (например, so 'bravīt, sa uvāca).
claims.number: DCS-2021 surface tokens: sa 24,813 + so 3,955 vs saḥ 1,482 → so/sa = 95.1% of the three (verify_claims_dcs.py OCH_sah_so_sa; 'sa' includes a small homograph residue — direction unaffected)
claims.note: One of the few places any of the graded grammars states a SURFACE-frequency fact — and the corpus confirms it at 19:1.
claims.mg_footnote: Поверхностные варианты по DCS-2021: sa — 24 813, so — 3 955, saḥ — 1 482 словоупотребления: 95 % случаев местоимение реально стоит в виде sa/so. «Чаще всего» Зализняка — ещё и преуменьшение.

claims.claim_ru: Наклонения: изъявительное, повелительное, желательное, а также сравнительно редко употребляемые прекатив, инъюнктив и кондиционалис.
claims.number: DCS-2021 tokens: benedictive-medium 221 + injunctive 2,366 + conditional 269 = 0.365% of verbal — vs optative 72,826 (9.3%), imperative 32,750 (4.2%), indicative present alone 157,003 (verify_claims_dcs.py OCH_rare_moods)
claims.note: The three-way split of the mood system into living core vs marginal triple is corpus-exact.
claims.mg_footnote: Частотность наклонений по DCS-2021: оптатив 72 826, императив 32 750 словоупотреблений — против прекатива (мед.) 221, инъюнктива 2 366 и кондиционалиса 269, вместе 0,37 % глагольных. «Сравнительно редко» — точная, даже мягкая формулировка.

claims.claim_ru: Смысловые различия между имперфектом, аористом и перфектом видны главным образом в ведах... В более поздний период все три формы становятся практически равнозначными.
claims.number: systemic: Whitney describes the classical preterits as interchangeable narrative pasts; corpus context: imperfect ~47,554 · perfect 61,986 · aorist 12,054 tokens (feat_formation; REFRESHED 17-07-2026 from the older 2,452 tense-code count, which undercounted root+thematic aorists, H1134/H1136) — semantic levelling with still-unequal frequencies (the aorist the rarest of the three)
claims.note: The instructive cross-grammar contrast: Zaliznyak asserts SEMANTIC equivalence only — exactly the half of Bühler's twin claim that survived verification (HB-61), without the frequency misstep that flagged Bühler's other half (HB-57). Kochergina's stratum-hedged version (HK-15) also stands. Three grammars, one fact, three calibrations — Zaliznyak's is the cleanest.

claims.claim_ru: Очень многие глаголы имеют только активные формы, значительное число — только медиальные.
claims.number: systemic: parasmaipada-only and ātmanepada-only conjugation is lexically fixed per root across the tradition's dhātupāṭha marking (P/Ā/U); the P-only class is the larger — cf. the Talmud catalog's pada column
claims.note: The Talmud catalog's pada column could turn this into an exact count in a future pass; the direction (P-only > A-only) matches its distribution and Bühler's deponent claim (HB-13) covers the middle-only half.

claims.claim_ru: Большинство глаголов с презенсом на ya непереходны и обозначают состояние.
claims.number: systemic: the ya-class core is the intransitive/affect set (nah, lubh, kup, muh, çam, tam, kṣudh... type) — Whitney characterizes the class accordingly; the transitive residue (nṛt? paç-) is small
claims.note: Proportion not corpus-measured (no transitivity tagging); the class characterization is the reference grammar's own.

claims.claim_ru: За пределами рядов I, U, R сильная и слабая основы презенса II в послеведийский период фактически различны только у √as, √vaç, √han, √çās.
claims.number: systemic: outside vowel-gradating roots (i/u/ṛ-series: eti/imaḥ, dveṣṭi/dviṣmaḥ...), the living alternators are exactly the suppletive/samprasāraṇa four — asti/santi, vaṣṭi/uçmasi, hanti/ghnanti, çāsti/çiṣṭaḥ
claims.ref: Whitney §§636 (as), 638 (han), 639 (çās); cf. OCH-4 and KnauerFrazy_1908 KN-3 (çās independently)
claims.note: A sharp closed-list universal that holds; two of its four members are already independently verified in this programme (çās twice, as via Bühler HB-harvest).

claims.claim_ru: Все корни, от которых в послеведийский период употребителен презенс III (их немногим более десятка), — полноизменяемые.
claims.number: systemic: the classical reduplicating class is a short list (dā, dhā, hu, bhṛ, mā, hā, bhī, jan-/jā?, pṛ, nij, vij, viṣ...) — a dozen-plus, each with strong/weak alternation (dadāti/dadati, juhoti/juhvati)
claims.note: One of the few numeric claims in the book ('немногим более десятка') — matches the reference inventory's size.

claims.claim_ru: Основы этого класса [VII] образуются только от полноизменяемых корней рядов I1, U1, R1, N1, оканчивающихся на шумную согласную.
claims.number: systemic: the nasal-infix class is built on obstruent-final roots with short i/u/ṛ/a-series nuclei (yuj, rudh, bhid, chid, añj, bhañj...) — Whitney's class inventory fits the restriction
claims.note: The structural restriction is real; Bühler's parallel description (Урок XLII) states the same class shape less precisely.

claims.claim_ru: Из производных глаголов каузативы образуют аорист типа 3, прочие (очень редко) — типа 5.
claims.number: systemic: the reduplicated aorist is the causative's aorist (abībhidat from bhedayati); other derived conjugations' aorists are marginal and iṣ-type where attested
claims.ref: Whitney §§861, 1046-1047; = BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 harvest (Урок XLV: 'Этот аорист (и никакой другой) образуется от корней X кл. и каузативов'); Talmud-2.1.6 § Аорист, Шаг 1 (H1054)
claims.note: Cross-grammar convergence with Bühler on both halves, including the rarity of the residue. TALMUD CROSS-CHECK (H1054): exact match — the Talmud's derived-stem rule assigns CAUS. ({2/3.pay}) to aorist class 3 and the remaining derived suffixes (other CAUS., DES., denominative) to class 5, i.e. both halves of Zaliznyak's rule as stated. One corner diverges: the Talmud sends INT. to class 4 (s-), not 5 — with intensive aorists attested at near-zero, a catalog refinement of «прочие», not a refutation.

claims.claim_ru: Аорист 3 обычно выступает как аорист каузатива, хотя и образуется непосредственно от корня.
claims.number: systemic: Whitney — the reduplicated aorist 'is in the later language the accepted aorist of the causative conjugation'; its non-causative uses are lexical relics
claims.note: The form-vs-function split ('образуется от корня' but functions as the causative's) is stated exactly as the reference grammar has it.

claims.claim_ru: В med. (употребляемом довольно редко) [аорист 4] обычно слабая ступень корня.
claims.number: grade half systemic (Whitney: middle s-aorist has the unstrengthened/weak root vs vṛddhied active); frequency half rests on Whitney's description — the DCS aorist voice codes (act 1,304 vs med 1,148 tokens across ALL aorist types) cannot isolate type 4, and the medium bucket is inflated by the common 3sg passive-aorist forms
claims.ref: Whitney §§878-881; DCS timws.csv codes 10-13 (instrument caveat recorded)
claims.note: Honest instrument note: the corpus export lumps aorist types and voices in a way that cannot check 'довольно редко' for type 4 specifically; the verdict rides on the systemic authority for both halves, with the code-lumping caveat recorded.

claims.claim_ru: Причастия прошедшего времени (иначе перфектные причастия) в послеведийский период употребляются редко.
claims.number: systemic: the perfect participle recedes in the classical language, largely lexicalized (vidvāṁs as an adjective); the productive past participles are the ta/na and tavat sets
claims.ref: Whitney §§802-807, 959-960; cf. BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-34 (the paradigm), HB-40 (the tavat competitor)
claims.note: Consistent with the corpus-side picture from the Bühler register: the ta-participle carries the past-participle load (29.8% of verbal tokens).

claims.claim_ru: Особенность: все корни на ar имеют соединительное i (независимо от деления aniṭ/seṭ) в основе простого будущего, например, kariṣya- от √kar.
claims.number: systemic: kariṣyati, hariṣyati, smariṣyati, bhariṣyati — the ṛ-final roots take i in the sya-future even where aniṭ elsewhere (kṛ: kartum but kariṣyati)
claims.ref: Whitney §§934-935; = BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 harvest (Урок XLIV line 6763: 'у корней на ṛ всегда вставляется i' — same universal, two grammars)
claims.note: A safe absolute independently stated by Bühler and Zaliznyak; Zaliznyak adds the sharper point (it cross-cuts the aniṭ/seṭ classification — kṛ itself is the demonstration).

claims.claim_ru: Встречается (очень редко) также med. [перифрастическое будущее II]... (в 3-м лице форм, отличных от act., нет).
claims.number: systemic (Whitney: the middle periphrastic future is rare; 3rd person = the bare agent noun for both voices); corpus side: DCS carries no medium code for the periphrastic future at all — its 1,290 tokens are all filed active
claims.note: The corpus's own coding (no medium bucket) is indirect confirmation of how marginal the middle is.

claims.claim_ru: Основным является суффикс -ta-; -na- выступает после большинства корней на ar (ряда R2) и на d, после части корней на ā, ī, ū и некоторых других.
claims.number: default half corpus-confirmed (-na = 8.0% of distinct PPP forms, -ta 92%); the subset half systemic — Whitney: na is taken by most roots in ṝ (tīrṇa, pūrṇa) and many in d (bhinna, chinna, sanna) plus part of the ā/ī/ū set
claims.ref: Whitney §957; DCS 12.csv (HK29/BU4 split); cf. KocherginaUchebnik_1998 HK-29, BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-38
claims.note: Fourth register appearance of the -ta/-na fact, and the most precise phrasing of the four: Zaliznyak names the exact root classes where -na wins, not just its minority status.

claims.claim_ru: Деепричастие на -tvā обычно можно получить из причастия на -ta- простой заменой суффикса; однако у производных глаголов ay не усекается.
claims.number: systemic: gata/gatvā, ukta/uktvā, kṛta/kṛtvā — same weak stem and same iṭ-treatment as a rule; derived verbs keep the formative (corayitvā vs corita)
claims.ref: Whitney §§990-991, 1051; = BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 harvest (Урок XXIX: aniṭ gerunds mirror aniṭ participles; -ayitvā always for causatives/class X)
claims.note: Both halves independently stated by Bühler; the ay-retention is HB-covered ('всегда -ayitvā') and Zaliznyak's 'обычно' correctly scopes the residue of mismatched doublets.

claims.claim_ru: Полноизменяемые открытые корни рядов M1, N1 обычно допускают два варианта: -gam-ya и -ga-tya от gam.
claims.number: systemic: -gamya/-gatya, -namya/-natya, -ramya/-ratya doublets are the reference description for the nasal-final short-vowel roots
claims.ref: Whitney §992; = BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 harvest (Урок XXIX line 4015: the same doublet list)
claims.note: Same doublets, two grammars — Bühler files them under 'неправильны', Zaliznyak derives them from series membership; the facts agree.

claims.claim_ru: Каузатив от переходного глагола довольно часто... имеет значение, практически равное значению исходного глагола (ср. kārayati).
claims.number: no instrument: adjudicating semantic equivalence of causative vs base tokens needs sense-annotated usage data; neither DCS's morphological export nor Whitney's formal description measures it
claims.note: Real and commonly noted (kārayati ≈ karoti in narrative usage); the quantifier 'довольно часто' is what has no measure.

claims.claim_ru: Ряд корней (многие на am, значительная часть корней seṭ на ar, al, an и некоторые другие) вместо ступени vṛddhi или наряду с ней имеют guṇa в каузативе (в раннем языке таких отступлений больше, чем в позднем).
claims.number: MEASURED with the causative detector built 16-07-2026 (causative_grade_detector.py, H1001): DCS lemmatizes causatives as their own -ay lemmas, so grade pairs (gamay~gāmay, smaray~smāray) are detected at lemma level, anchored on the root lemma existing in the same DB (screens denominatives), and sliced by the H1000 period map. 150 anchored pairs, 57 fluctuating (both grades attested). The guṇa share FALLS exactly as §167 predicts — all pairs: 45.8% (веды) → 20.9% (эпос) → 15.4% (классика); fluctuating pairs, where the choice is actually visible: 81.3% → 25.9% → 20.8% — a fourfold collapse. Both directions monotonic; the purāṇa control again sits at epic level (26.3%). Synchronic membership confirmed en passant: gam/jan/tvar are guṇa-fixed (Whitney §1042's set), kṛ/mṛ/dhṛ vṛddhi-fixed, exactly as the § describes
claims.note: Closed by the two-instrument chain the register itself specified: H1000's period map plus this pass's causative detector. The measured contrast is stark enough that the §'s cautious parenthesis («в раннем языке таких отступлений больше») reads as understatement: in the Vedic slice the 'deviation' was the MAJORITY option (81%) among fluctuating roots. Detector caveat kept honest: -ay lemmas conflate causative and class-10/denominative morphology; root-anchoring screens most denominatives, and grade CONTRAST within one root is causative territory regardless.

claims.claim_ru: У корней группы √pā/pāy (§ 70, п. 4) каузатив обычно типа pāyayati.
claims.number: systemic: pāyayati (with the āy-glide) is the accepted causative of pā 'drink'; the p-causative belongs to other ā-roots (sthāpayati, dāpayati)
claims.ref: Whitney §1042 (the pāyayati type); cf. BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-26/HB-27 (the ā-root causative treatments)
claims.note: 'Обычно' correctly leaves room for the marginal pāpayati variant the tradition records.

claims.claim_ru: У корней со срединным i, u, ar [в дезидеративе] слабая ступень перед sa, но обычно guṇa перед iṣa; у прочих корней, как правило, guṇa.
claims.number: systemic: bibhitsati / didṛkṣati type (weak before sa) vs cikartiṣati-type guṇa before iṣa — Whitney's desiderative-grade description matches both branches
claims.note: A two-conditional rule with two hedges ('обычно', 'как правило'), each sitting on the attested variation.

claims.claim_ru: Формы [дезидератива] за пределами презенса (довольно редкие) — от основы вида jijñās-, didṛkṣ- и т.д.
claims.number: systemic: the desiderative lives in its present system; the further tenses are sparsely attested and built on the sa-stem as stated
claims.ref: Whitney §§1032-1034; = BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 harvest (Урок XLVIII line 7211: 'одно только [настоящее] встречается довольно часто' — same claim, same direction)
claims.note: Cross-grammar convergence with Bühler on the same rarity distribution.

claims.claim_ru: В классическом языке интенсивы употребляются довольно редко, причем преимущественно в med. и практически только в презенсе.
claims.number: systemic: the classical intensive is dominated by the ya-formation (middle by construction — bobhūyate), with the active bare-reduplicated type receding; non-present intensive forms are 'крайне редко' per the reference description
claims.ref: Whitney §§1000-1019 (esp. 1016-1019); = BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-63 (the two-type voice split) + harvest line 7325 (non-present forms 'крайне редко')
claims.note: All three parts match Bühler's independent account once his two-type split is read against Zaliznyak's aggregate: the surviving productive type is the middle one, hence 'преимущественно в med.'.

claims.claim_ru: Вне презенса [у деноминативов] употребительны только неличные формы, причем почти исключительно при основе на aya.
claims.number: no instrument: the DCS export does not tag denominatives as a class, so their non-present/non-finite distribution cannot be isolated; Whitney's treatment does not state this specific distribution
claims.ref: cf. Whitney §§1053-1068 (denominatives, no distributional statement of this shape)
claims.note: Plausibly drawn from Zaliznyak's own reading; needs a denominative-tagged form inventory to check.

claims.claim_ru: [Суффиксы -as-, -man- как имена действия] Имена деятеля редки.
claims.number: systemic: -as- and -man- are the action/abstract-noun suffixes of the reference description; their agentive items are lexical exceptions, not a productive reading
claims.note: Safe minority claim about derivational semantics the reference grammar states the same way.

claims.claim_ru: В целом у прилагательных преобладает -ā- (только -ā- у причастий), у существительных — -ī-.
claims.number: systemic, scoped to masculines in -a (source-checked): gata- → gatā- (never *gatī-) for participles; the -ant participles' -antī feminines belong to a different stem class and are handled by Zaliznyak's own next paragraph
claims.ref: Whitney §§332-334 (ā/ī feminines of a-stems); ZalizniakOcherk mdx §180 (scope confirmed against the source)
claims.note: The seemingly risky absolute ('только -ā- у причастий') is safe once the §'s scope — masculine stems in -a — is read: it covers gata-type participles, not the ant-stems, which the same § treats separately with their three-group ī-feminine rule.

claims.claim_ru: У имен на -aka- женский род обычно на -ikā-: pācaka- — pācikā-.
claims.number: systemic: the pācaka-/pācikā- alternation is the standing pattern (kāraka-/kārikā-, nartaka-/nartakī- as the hedged residue)
claims.note: 'Обычно' covers the -akī residue; the dominant pattern is as stated.

claims.claim_ru: При мужском роде на i, as, а также у корневых имен женский род обычно совпадает с мужским.
claims.number: systemic: çuci- m=f, sumanas- m=f, root compounds m=f — the two-ending adjective classes of the reference description
claims.note: Matches Bühler's parallel statements (his Урок IV/XXII adjective paragraphs) and Whitney.

claims.claim_ru: Реже встречаются (обычно в паре друг с другом) суффиксы -īyaṁs- (сравнит.) и -iṣṭha- (превосходн.); их значения практически те же, что у -tara-, -tama-.
claims.number: systemic: the primary comparison is a closed lexical class of a few dozen root-attached pairs; -tara/-tama is the open productive formation
claims.ref: Whitney §§466-474; = BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-48 (the same claim, verified there 14-07-2026 — direct cross-book reuse, as the harvest note anticipated)
claims.note: The 'verify once, promote to both books' plan from this book's own harvest header is hereby executed: HB-48's verification carries this entry, and the pairing observation ('обычно в паре') matches the suppletive pair lists both books give.

claims.claim_ru: Несколько особый (сравнительно редко) случай karmadhāraya составляют сложные слова с количественным числительным (кроме eka-) в первой части [dvigu].
claims.number: MEASURED with the structural compound tagger built 16-07-2026 (compound_type_tagger.py, H1004): over 623,119 nominal compound clusters (DCS mwt spans, upos-classed members), dvigu candidates (NUM-first, eka excluded per the §'s own parenthesis: 1,875 eka-first counted apart) number 16,406 = 15.3% of the determinative-shaped pool (ADJ+NUM first, 107,044) — 1:5.4 against plain adjective-first — and just 2.6% of all nominal clusters. Top dvigu numerals: tri 2,595 · dvi 1,476 · saptan 1,445 · pañcan 1,394 · catur 1,259. «Сравнительно редко» holds as a clear minority subtype
claims.ref: ZalizniakOcherk_1978/och58_compound_stats.json; compound_type_tagger.py; the parallel Bühler Урок-XXXIII dvigu candidates (claims_harvest.yml) are UNBLOCKED by the same instrument for a future drain
claims.note: The tagger is function-blind by design (bahuvrīhis of the same ADJ/NUM-first shape sit inside the pool — the very limitation OCH-60 documents), but the contamination applies to both shapes alike, so the comparative claim survives it. The §'s eka-exclusion is honored structurally: eka-first clusters counted separately, not as dvigu.

claims.claim_ru: Основы на an, выступая в конце сложного слова, довольно часто заменяются основами на a, например: mahā-rāja- (ср. rājan-).
claims.number: systemic: -rāja-, -ahna-, -akṣa- type compound finals are the standing pattern; Bühler states the tatpuruṣa case as near-universal ('rājan изменяется в rāja'), Zaliznyak's 'довольно часто' covers the wider compound field where -an finals also survive
claims.ref: Whitney §1315 (a-stem substitutes in composition); cf. BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 harvest (Урок XXXIV line 4757)
claims.note: Two grammars, two scopes, one fact: the narrower the scope (Bühler's tatpuruṣa-final), the stronger the quantifier can safely be.

claims.claim_ru: Омонимия первичных и вторичных сложных имен... практически встречается реже, чем можно было бы ожидать, поскольку за многими словами закрепляется одна из этих двух функций.
claims.number: no instrument, now PROBED rather than presumed (16-07-2026, H1004): DCS tags compound members LEXICALLY — mahābāhu, a textbook bahuvrīhi, carries last-member upos NOUN in 192/193 clusters — so tatpuruṣa/bahuvrīhi FUNCTION is not recoverable from this snapshot at all; per-token function disambiguation (or Vedic accent, which classical orthography loses) remains the missing instrument
claims.ref: cf. Whitney §1293ff (the accentual distinction that classical orthography loses); probe recorded in och58_compound_stats.json (och60_probe key)
claims.note: An expectation-vs-usage claim that is exactly right in kind but needs a disambiguated compound corpus to measure.

claims.claim_ru: Изредка в качестве первого члена [сложного слова] выступает не основа, а словоформа какого-либо падежа, например: dhanaṁ-jaya-.
claims.number: systemic: the aluk type (dhanaṁjaya-, vācaspati-, manasija-) is a small lexicalized set against the default stem-composition
claims.ref: Whitney §1250 (case-forms as prior members); cf. BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 harvest (Урок XXXIII line 4673 — the same 'иногда' claim)
claims.note: Third convergent hedge on the same fact ('изредка' / 'иногда'); the type is closed and enumerable.

claims.claim_ru: Активная конструкция очень часто заменяется пассивной... В прошедшем времени такая замена обычно сопровождается переходом к именному предложению.
claims.number: corpus: finite ya-passive ≈ 3.9% of verbal tokens (present passive alone 24,949) and the ta-participle — the past nominal-sentence predicate — is the single most frequent verbal category of the corpus (233,080 tokens = 29.8%)
claims.ref: DCS-2021 timws.csv codes 19, 24-27, 29 (numbers reused from BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-19/HB-39)
claims.note: The syntactic claim whose corpus shadow is fully visible without a treebank: the ta-participle's 29.8% share IS the nominal-past pattern Zaliznyak describes.
claims.mg_footnote: Числа по DCS-2021: личные формы пассива ≈ 3,9 % глагольных словоупотреблений, а причастие на -ta — ядро именного предложения прошедшего времени — 233 080 словоупотреблений (29,8 % всех глагольных, чаще настоящего времени). Оба звена утверждения Зализняка — «очень часто» и «обычно через именное предложение» — стоят прямо на статистическом центре корпуса.

claims.claim_ru: В ведах и в раннем послеведийском языке преобладают словосочетания, глагольные предложения, активные конструкции (синтаксический стиль I). В позднем классическом санскрите, напротив, господствуют сложные слова, именные предложения, пассивные конструкции (синтаксический стиль II).
claims.number: MEASURED with the period-tagging instrument built 16-07-2026 (period_style_gradient.py, H1000: curated 41-text period map over the pinned DCS SQLite, 4.07M tokens = 71.6% of the corpus). ALL FIVE style metrics rise monotonically vedic → epic → classical exactly as §207 predicts: compound membership 12.2% → 40.5% → 57.3% of nominal tokens (near-5×), mean compound length 2.06 → 2.32 → 2.58 members, nominal token share 43.3% → 52.7% → 56.4%, noun/verb ratio 2.13 → 2.89 → 3.28, finite-passive share 2.7% → 5.8% → 7.5% (this last conditional on DCS's sparse verbal annotation; the other four are annotation-robust upos/segmentation metrics). Internal control: the purāṇas, bucketed separately because their verse imitates epic style, land BETWEEN epic and classical on the compound axis (48.1%) — the map measures language, not genre labels
claims.ref: ZalizniakOcherk_1978/och63_period_style_stats.json (per-text table + validation); period_style_gradient.py (period map with dating basis per text)
claims.note: The register's FLAGSHIP open item, CLOSED as the programme's third headline finding: the style-I → style-II shift is not just real, it is the strongest gradient this programme has measured (the compound axis nearly quintuples). Honesty boundaries kept: Buddhist-hybrid texts and lexica excluded as register-confounded; feats-based metrics (finite-verb rate, ta-participle rate) EXCLUDED from the verdict because DCS verbal annotation density collapses for later texts (measured: tagged ta-participles RV 1,874 → Daśakumāracarita 0) — the verdict rests only on annotation-robust axes. A60-adjacent paper material.

claims.claim_ru: Предлоги развиты в санскрите слабо. Слов, которые выполняли бы только функцию предлогов, практически нет.
claims.number: systemic: Whitney's 'prepositions' chapter opens on the same point — the case-governing words are adverbs/preverbs in secondary use; no dedicated prepositional class exists
claims.note: The reference grammar makes the identical typological point; the hedge 'практически' covers the near-grammaticalized items (ā with ablative).

claims.claim_ru: Случаи, где prati и т. п. выступают в бесспорно предложной функции, относительно более редки.
claims.number: MEASURED with the treebank instrument built 16-07-2026 (treebank_syntax_stats.py, H1022): dcs_full.sqlite's own head/deprel columns carry a genuine, fully-tagged UD-style slice (223,751 tokens, 3.9% of the corpus, 29,433 complete sentences) — the prior "no instrument, the export lacks syntactic annotation" premise was never actually checked. Among 143 dependency-tagged lemma='prati' tokens, only 29 (20.3%) carry the unambiguous adpositional deprel (`case`); the rest are adverbial (`advmod`, 91), part of a fixed multiword unit (`flat`, 14), or other (9). 20.3% is a clear minority
claims.note: Direction and magnitude both confirm 'относительно более редки' cleanly; unaffected by the genre-skew investigation below (OCH-66/67).

claims.claim_ru: Глагольное сказуемое... тяготеет к последнему месту в предложении, группа подлежащего — к первому... Определяющее слово обычно предшествует определяемому.
claims.number: MEASURED (treebank_syntax_stats.py, H1022) over the same 29,433-sentence slice. Of the THREE tendencies packed into this one claim, only one is robustly confirmed: modifier-precedes-head, 86.0% (22,142 det/amod/nmod/nummod edges) — holds equally in a post-Vedic "classical" subset (Mahābhārata/Manusmṛti/Arthaśāstra/Rāmāyaṇa/ Buddhacarita, 86.5%) and the Vedic/Śrauta-sūtra remainder (86.0%). The other two do NOT clear even a loose majority: verb-final 46.4% overall (classical 37.6%, vedic/ sūtra 46.8%; Arthaśāstra alone, the single largest non-metrical classical-prose sample, 51.4%); subject-group-first 42.6% overall (classical 46.4%, vedic/sūtra 42.4%). GENRE-SKEW CHECKED, not assumed: a classical-vs-Vedic split and an Arthaśāstra-only isolation both rule out "Vedic-skewed corpus" as the explanation — neither sub-claim clears 52% in ANY split tested. Two operationalization robustness checks (excluding trailing discourse-particles from the "last token" test: 46.4%→48.4%; restricting to single-clause sentences only, to rule out a later clause dragging the sentence-final position away from the root's own clause: 46.4%→51.1%) each moved the number under 5 points — not an artifact of the metric
claims.note: The hedges themselves ('тяготеет'/'обычно', not 'всегда') are appropriately soft IF the underlying tendency were real — but on this instrument two of the three packed claims read as closer to free word order than a lean either way. This is the standard Indological consensus description of Classical Sanskrit typology, and a 3.9% DCS slice dominated by Vedic Saṃhitā/Brāhmaṇa/Śrauta-sūtra prose plus a thin post-Vedic tail may simply be under-powered or genre-mismatched for testing it — that uncertainty is real and not resolved here, but the number as measured does not support the claim as stated for two of its three parts.

claims.claim_ru: Из сложных предложений в санскрите преобладают сложносочиненные.
claims.number: MEASURED (treebank_syntax_stats.py, H1022). Among sentences with 2+ FINITE verbs (feat_verbform IS NULL, mood-marked — verified against feat_mood; DCS's raw acl/ advcl/ccomp/xcomp deprels are dominated by NON-finite participial/absolutive embedding, §217's actual preferred mechanism, which is not a 'сложноподчинённое предложение' in the traditional finite-clause sense and was excluded from both sides of this count): **419 coordinate (`conj`) vs 2,739 finite-subordinate (`advcl`/`acl`/`ccomp`/`xcomp`) — subordinate outnumbers coordinate 6.5:1, the OPPOSITE of the claimed direction.** GENRE-SKEW CHECKED: the classical subset shows meaningfully MORE coordination proportionally (29 coordinate vs 58 subordinate = 33.3% coordinate share) than the Vedic/Śrauta-sūtra remainder (390 vs 2,681 = 12.7% share) — a real, measurable shift in the expected direction — but even the classical subset's 33.3% falls well short of the >50% the claim requires
claims.note: The genre split shows the corpus skew is a REAL, partial contributor (coordination share roughly triples in classical texts) but does not reverse the inversion — subordinate clauses (chiefly relative acl:rel and complement ccomp) outnumber coordinate ones in every split tested, including the classical-only one. Whether this reflects DCS's specific text selection within "classical" (Mahābhārata is itself compilation/didactic-heavy) or a genuine correction to the traditional description is not resolved here — recorded as measured, not settled linguistic theory.

claims.claim_ru: Существительное, к которому относится пара ya- ... ta- ..., всегда стоит в первом из двух предложений.
claims.number: MEASURED (treebank_syntax_stats.py, H1022). Restricted to acl:rel edges (relative- clause-verb -> head noun) where (a) the head is a genuine NOUN, not a bare correlative pronoun (excludes cases like 'sa/tad' where no lexical antecedent is shared — those don't test this claim), and (b) the relative clause's own subtree contains a PRON lemma 'yad' (confirms a genuine yad-relative, not another acl:rel type): **n = 335, head noun in the linearly-first clause in 252 = 75.2%.** A quarter of a 335-case sample (83 counterexamples) falls short of "всегда." Manually inspected several counterexamples against the raw token sequence to sanity-check the clause-boundary logic before trusting this number — the operationalization holds up
claims.note: Flagged in advance (before any number existed) as the register's one absolute quantifier sitting on an unmeasured syntactic domain — exactly the kind of claim a corpus test could most plausibly dent, and it did. 75.2% is a real majority (the general tendency is genuine) but 'всегда' oversells it by a wide margin on n=335; this is the sharpest OVERSTATED case in the whole Ocherk register precisely because it uses the strongest possible quantifier on the thinnest-margin claim.

claims.claim_ru: Сложноподчиненные предложения встречаются... в санскрите гораздо реже, чем, например, в русском или в латыни.
claims.number: no instrument: a cross-language subordination-density comparison needs comparable parsed corpora on both sides — out of scope for this pipeline's ground truth
claims.note: The direction is the consensus typological picture (absolutives + compounds do the subordination work); the comparison itself is unmeasured here.

claims.claim_ru: Так, вместо косвенной речи почти всегда применяется прямая речь.
claims.number: systemic: the iti-construction is the standing reported-speech device of the language; grammaticalized indirect speech is marginal — Whitney's description of iti carries the same generalization
claims.note: 'Почти всегда' matches the iti-device's monopoly; the corpus's ubiquitous iti-tokens are its surface shadow.

claims.claim_ru: Небольшая группа словоформ выступает только в качестве энклитик [ca, vā, iva, u, ha, sma, svit; mā, me, tvā, te, naḥ, vaḥ, nau, vām; все формы ena-].
claims.number: systemic: the particle set (ca, vā, iva, u, ha, sma, svit), the unaccented pronoun forms, and enad's forms are the accentless inventory of the reference description
claims.ref: Whitney §§491b (pronoun enclitics), 500 (enad), 1122 (accentless particles); = BuhlerLeitfaden_1923 HB-30 (the pronoun half, verified) + OCH-seed's enad anaphora (HB-37 twin)
claims.note: Zaliznyak's list is the union of three already-verified sublists across the programme's registers — closed, and cross-book consistent.

claims.claim_ru: Как правило, безударны две важных категории словоформ: а) все личные формы глагола без преверба... б) все словоформы вокатива.
claims.number: systemic: the accentlessness of the finite verb in an independent clause and of the non-initial vocative is Whitney's own headline accent rule — with the same exception structure ('как правило': clause-initial position, subordinate clauses) Zaliznyak's hedge reserves
claims.ref: Whitney §§591-593 (verb accent, incl. the subordinate-clause exception §595), §314 (vocative)
claims.note: The programme's third calibration data point on this exact fact: Kochergina's absolute «личная форма глагола никогда не несет ударения» graded OVERSTATED (HK-10 — the subordinate clause accents it); Zaliznyak's «как правило» owns the exceptions and grades TRUE. Same fact, opposite verdicts, hedging is the whole difference.

claims.claim_ru: Остальная парадигма в подавляющем большинстве случаев... имеет колонное ударение.
claims.number: systemic: fixed accent is the default of Vedic declension; mobile accent is confined to the monosyllabic stems and a few consonant-stem classes
claims.note: The complementary half of OCH-74; both sides of the split are the reference description.

claims.claim_ru: Неколонное ударение представлено лишь у меньшинства имен.
claims.number: systemic: the mobile-accent classes (monosyllables, some ancient consonant stems) are a closed archaic minority against the fixed-accent bulk
claims.note: Consistent restatement of OCH-73's split from the other side; no counter-inventory exists.

claims.claim_ru: Средний залог показывает (в наиболее ясных случаях), что действие направлено на сам субъект или производится для него, в его интересах.
claims.number: no instrument: per-token benefactivity/reflexivity is untagged (same wall as HB-100); the hedge «в наиболее ясных случаях» already concedes the fuzzy zone
claims.note: The guarded twin of Bühler's HB-100 «главное значение» — Zaliznyak hedges what Bühler states flatly.

claims.claim_ru: Нередко действительный и средний залоги употребляются без различия значения.
claims.number: no instrument: sense-difference tagging of doublets absent

claims.claim_ru: Противопоставление среднего и страдательного залогов возможно лишь в части форм.
claims.number: corpus-consistent: dedicated Pass tokens sit 97.9% in the present system (Pres 34,499 + Impf 1,443 of 36,701) with a small Past bucket 759 (the -i aorist and residuals) — outside it no dedicated passive morphology exists (och_voice_stats.json passive_by_tense)
claims.ref: och_voice_stats.py; Whitney ya-passive (present system) + 3sg aorist passive

claims.claim_ru: Даже морфологически недвусмысленные формы страдательного залога нередко допускают медиальное значение наряду с собственно пассивным.
claims.number: no instrument: per-token diathesis-SENSE (middle vs passive value of a ya-form) untagged

claims.claim_ru: Пассив. Имеется система презенса пассива и одна форма аориста.
claims.number: passive_by_tense: Pres 34,499 (94.0%) · Impf 1,443 (3.9%) · Past 759 (2.1%) — the Past bucket carries the 3sg -i aorist type (DCS folds aorists under Tense=Past); no Fut/Perf dedicated passive rows exist
claims.note: The Tense=Past mapping conflates preterites (A65 EM2), so 759 is an upper bound for the aorist-passive — direction unaffected. TALMUD CROSS-CHECK (H1054): exact confirmation — «AOR.PASS. образует только форму Ā.3.SG.» (Talmud-2.1.6 § Аорист, Шаг 3), i.e. precisely Zaliznyak's «одна форма аориста».

claims.claim_ru: Особый случай: krāma- (обычно в act.) и krama- (обычно в med.) от √kram.
claims.number: measured: krāma- forms 84 P vs 4 Ā; krama- forms 94 Ā vs 7 P (och_voice_stats.json kram_grade_voice) — both «обычно» hold at 95%/93%
claims.note: A textbook-perfect hedge: both directions of the split measured within a few tokens of exceptionless.

claims.claim_ru: Большинство глаголов с презенсом на ya непереходны и обозначают состояние.
claims.number: no instrument: transitivity untagged in the snapshot; a valency lexicon join is the missing tool
claims.ref: transitivity wall (shared with OCH-93's quantified reading)

claims.claim_ru: Med. [у √i] только в adhi-i «учиться».
claims.number: bare-lemma i finite middles are marginal: 30 of 2,655 classifiable tokens (1.1%), and the list is Vedic prayers (īmahe 11) plus untagged ya-passives (īyate/īyase) and future middles — no post-Vedic plain middle present of bare i; adhi-i is lemmatized separately (adhī, adhīte)
claims.note: Post-Vedic scope per the Ocherk's own frame; the Vedic īmahe residue sits outside it.

claims.claim_ru: √as «быть»: Med. только в составе будущего II (§ 159).
claims.number: lemma as: 21,014 P-classified finite tokens vs 9 Ā-classified — and the 9 are as₂ 'throw' class-IV forms (asyatām 7, asyate 1) plus one ās- 'sit' artifact (āse); genuine middles of as 'be' outside future II: 0 in the classifiable mass
claims.note: DCS lemma 'as' conflates as 'be' and as 'throw' — the middle residue dissolves into the homonym, which the form list makes explicit.

claims.claim_ru: √çī «лежать» (только med.).
claims.number: measured: 487 Ā-classified finite tokens (śete 273, śerate 66, śayīta 113 …) vs 5 P-classified stragglers (99.0% Ā; the 5 are classifier noise at the sandhi floor)

claims.claim_ru: [Аорист 1 (корневой)] в послеведийский период возможен только у корней на ā и у √bhū, только в act.
claims.number: systemic: the classical root-aorist inventory narrows to the ā-class + abhūt, parasmaipada — the exact carve-out whose absence made Bühler's HB-2 voice-symmetry claim OVERSTATED; corpus cannot isolate aorist type (formation untagged, A65 EM2)
claims.note: Note the scope discipline vs HK-38: Kochergina states the same restriction WITHOUT the post-Vedic scoping and lands OVERSTATED; Zaliznyak scopes it and lands TRUE. TALMUD CROSS-CHECK (H1054): the Talmud (Vedic-inclusive) assigns class 1 to 142 of 745 roots — the breadth of the base inventory from which Zaliznyak's post-Vedic «только -ā и bhū, только act.» is the narrowing; on the diachronic narrowing itself the Talmud is silent, which is exactly why the scoping decides the verdict here vs HK-38.

claims.claim_ru: [Аорист 5 (iṣ-)] Ступень корня — в act. guṇa или vṛddhi, в med. всегда guṇa.
claims.number: systemic per Whitney's iṣ-aorist description (middle with guṇa; vṛddhi confined to the active); corpus cannot isolate the type (formation untagged)

claims.claim_ru: [Аорист 6 (siṣ-)] Имеется только act. (в med. у тех же корней выступает аорист 4).
claims.number: systemic: the siṣ-aorist is active-only in the standard description — the second HB-2 carve-out stated explicitly here
claims.ref: Whitney §§911-916 (siṣ-aorist); Талмуд — та же прекативная оговорка (класс 6 в Ā-прекативе «еще реже», в med. те же корни обслуживает класс 4) — [Талмуд-сверка H1054](https://github.com/gasyoun/SanskritGrammar/blob/main/TolchelnikovTalmud_2026/AORIST_REGISTRY_TALMUD_CROSSCHECK_2026.md)

claims.claim_ru: В act. он [прекатив] не зависит, а в med. лишь отчасти зависит от того, какой тип аориста имеется у данного корня.
claims.number: systemic: the active precative is built directly on the weak root + yā(s)- regardless of the root's aorist type; the middle rides the s-aorist stem
claims.note: TALMUD CROSS-CHECK (H1054): exact confirmation of both halves — AOR.OPT. P. attaches {yās} to AOR.1 (the bare root-aorist stem) for ALL roots regardless of their own aorist class («не зависит»), while Ā. attaches {ī} to AOR.4, реже AOR.5, еще реже AOR.6 («лишь отчасти зависит») — the Talmud even supplies the productivity ranking.

claims.claim_ru: Пассив морфологически независим от соответствующих форм act. и med. При спряжении пассив принимает окончания med.
claims.number: systemic + corpus-consistent: the -yá- passive stem is built on the weak root independently of the present class, inflected with middle endings (uc-ya-te vs vak-ti)

claims.claim_ru: [Ведийский пассив отличался от med. IV класса ударением (pacyáte/pácyate).] В послеведийском языке такие формы омонимичны, а иногда почти неразличимы и по смыслу.
claims.number: systemic: the contrast was accentual (pacyáte pass. vs pácyate med.); classical texts carry no accent, so the forms are segmentally identical by construction
claims.note: The semantic half («иногда почти неразличимы») shares OCH-78's sense-tagging wall, but the homonymy core is definitional.

claims.claim_ru: У каузативов пассив образуется от усеченной основы — без конечного ay: bodhyate.
claims.number: systemic + attested: bodhyate, kāryate type — the aya-suffix drops before the passive -ya

claims.claim_ru: Во всех остальных личных формах для передачи пассивного значения используется просто med.; в очень редких случаях встречаются формы, образованные от основы 3 sg аориста пассива.
claims.number: no instrument for the core: passive SENSE of morphologically middle forms (cakre 'был сделан') is untagged; the rare pass-aorist-stem finites are below any reliable surface probe
claims.ref: sense-tagging wall (OCH-78 family); Талмуд закрывает системную половину (пассив аориста = только Ā.3.SG по построению), частотная половина остается UNTESTABLE — [Талмуд-сверка H1054](https://github.com/gasyoun/SanskritGrammar/blob/main/TolchelnikovTalmud_2026/AORIST_REGISTRY_TALMUD_CROSSCHECK_2026.md)

claims.claim_ru: Фактически они [причастия на -ta-/-na-] имеют пассивное значение только у переходных глаголов (sthita-, mṛta- — активное; gata- — двоякое).
claims.number: systemic: the standard doctrine — ta-participles of intransitives are active-valued (gata- 'ушедший', sthita-, mṛta-), of transitives passive-valued
claims.note: The per-token quantified reading would need transitivity tagging (OCH-81's wall); verdicted on the systemic rule, which Whitney states in the same terms.
